HEARING ON APPLICANT/PLAINTIFF JULIA KELLER-MCINTYRE'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE APPLICANT'S INCOME IS ENOUGH TO PAY THE COMMON NECESSARIES OF LIFE AND COURT FEES AND COSTS. FEE WAIVER DENIED. JULIA KELLER-MCINTYRE HAS TEN DAYS TO PAY THE FILING FEES OR THE FILINGS WILL BE STRICKEN. ORDER DENYING WAIVER OF FEE SENT TO ALL PARTIES. JUDGE: A. LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND ON ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT- DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ANY AND ALL COMMON LAW CLAIMS FOR FAILURE TO PLEAD SUBMISSION OF A TORT CLAIM OR EXHAUSTION OF REMEDIES. OTHERWISE OVERRULED BECAUSE AS DEFENDANT CONCEDES, THE STATUTORY CLAIMS ARE BASED IN PART ON ACTIONS TAKEN BY DEFENDANT SUBSEQUENT TO THE CONDUCT ALREADY ADJUDICATED IN FEDERAL COURT. WHILE PLAINTIFF CANNOT RELITIGATE CLAIMS BASED ON THAT CONDUCT, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED BECAUSE SUCH CONDUCT COULD HAVE RELEVANCE TO ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES CONCERNING THE LATER CONDUCT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB)

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF JULIA KELLER-MCINTYRE'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS: (1) THE MOTION DOES NOT CONTAIN A SEPARATE STATEMENT AND REQUESTS THE COURT TO ADVISE PLAINTIFF WHICH QUESTIONS ARE PROPER. (2) DISCOVERY HAS PROPOUNDED TO DEFENDANTS WHO HAVE NOT BEEN SERVED. (3) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADEQUATELY MET AND CONFERRED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Jun-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Julia Keller-McIntyre's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery is denied. The motion is denied for the following reasons: (1) The motion does not contain a separate statement and requests the court to advise Plaintiff which questions are proper. (2) Discovery has propounded to Defendants who have not been served. (3) Plaintiff has not adequately met and conferred. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- JULIA KELLER MCINTYRE, TEL. NO. 319-5204, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOHN SHUSE, TEL. NO. (650) 341-3693 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND ON ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT- DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ANY AND ALL COMMON LAW CLAIMS FOR FAILURE TO PLEAD SUBMISSION OF A TORT CLAIM OR EXHAUSTION OF REMEDIES. OTHERWISE OVERRULED BECAUSE AS DEFENDANT CONCEDES, THE STATUTORY CLAIMS ARE BASED IN PART ON ACTIONS TAKEN BY DEFENDANT SUBSEQUENT TO THE CONDUCT ALREADY ADJUDICATED IN FEDERAL COURT. WHILE PLAINTIFF CANNOT RELITIGATE CLAIMS BASED ON THAT CONDUCT, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED BECAUSE SUCH CONDUCT COULD HAVE RELEVANCE TO ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES CONCERNING THE LATER CONDUCT. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 =301/PJB/AL
